headline: 'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
description: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

headline: Xi, at center of global affairs, keeps Putin waiting on Power of Siberia 2 pipeline
Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
Israel faces European backlash over Gaza flotilla detentions
description: Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir sparked condemnation on Wednesday after releasing videos showing detained activists from a Gaza-bound flotilla, with their hands bound and foreheads on the ground.

headline: Who is Emmanuel Moulin, the new governor of France's central bank?
description: Parliament approved the appointment of the former Elysée chief of staff to head the Banque de France on Wednesday, May 20. Opponents criticized his closeness to both Nicolas Sarkozy and Emmanuel Macron.

headline: Sweden and France become privileged defense partners
description: Stockholm announced, on May 19, that it wanted to acquire four defense and intervention frigates from the French company Naval Group. The order shows Sweden's growing strategic ties with Paris, facilitated by its NATO accession.

headline: 'Gaza has become a diplomatic, political and military black hole'
description: Donald Trump's promises for the territory, devastated after two years of war, have continued to be proven wrong in the months following the ceasefire with Israel, writes Gilles Paris, columnist for Le Monde, in his column.

Robin Campillo, filmmaker: 'I don't see how a blacklist of French cinema could be enforced. Would it even be legal?'
Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

headline: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
description: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

headline: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
description: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.
